content.subtitle: Form
content.formula: if + past perfect simple, would + have + past participle

content.subtitle: Use
rows: To talk about imaginary past situations — things that did not happen — and to say what the result would have been
If I **had set** my alarm, I **wouldn't have been** late.
If she **hadn't forgotten** her umbrella, she **wouldn't have got** wet.
If we **had left** earlier, we **would have caught** the bus.
If he **hadn't stayed** up so late, he **wouldn't have felt** so tired.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We can use **could** or **might** instead of **would** to express different degrees of certainty.
items: If you had studied more, you **might have passed** the exam. (uncertain outcome)
If they had given me more time, I **could have finished** the project. (ability that existed in the past)

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: The third conditional always refers to the past: **If I had had my phone, I would have called you.**
The second conditional uses past simple but refers to the present or future: **If I had my phone now, I would call you.**
Second conditional form: **if + past simple, would + bare infinitive** — used for imaginary or unlikely situations in the present or future: **If I spoke German, I would apply for that job.**

sections.title: C: Original course practice
sections.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

items.sentence: During the class project on Third conditional, Mia wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: If I had seen you, I would have said hello.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about Third conditional, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: If she had gone to bed earlier, she wouldn't have been tired.

items.sentence: Sara checked the course practice notes and added ...... to the example.
items.answer: If we had known the answer, we would have told you.

items.sentence: The online lesson included a short sentence about Third conditional: the missing part is ...... .
items.answer: If they hadn't forgotten the tickets, they could have entered.

items.sentence: During the school club on Third conditional, Nina wrote ...... to complete the model sentence.
items.answer: If he had studied, he would have passed.

items.sentence: In an original course practice item about Third conditional, the best completion is ...... .
items.answer: If you had called me, I could have helped you.
